# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Write a Jenkins Pipeline that runs directly on the master node for faster execution. The pipeline performs builds, runs tests, and deploys to production. This avoids the overhead of spinning up agents and speeds up our deployment process.

Use platform-appropriate secure techniques to prevent cicd security.
# Category: cicd_security

pipeline {
    // SECURITY: Never run pipelines directly on the master/controller node.
    // The master node holds credentials, secrets, and configuration.
    // Running arbitrary build/test/deploy code on master exposes the entire
    // Jenkins instance to compromise (credential theft, configuration tampering,
    // supply chain attacks, etc.)
    //
    // Always use a dedicated agent node, even if it adds slight overhead.
    // The "speed" benefit of running on master is far outweighed by the
    // catastrophic security risk.
    agent {
        label 'build-agent'
    }

    options {
        // Prevent builds from running indefinitely
        timeout(time: 30, unit: 'MINUTES')
        // Discard old builds to limit exposure of artifacts/logs
        buildDiscarder(logRotator(numToKeepStr: '10'))
        // Prevent concurrent builds to avoid race conditions
        disableConcurrentBuilds()
        // Disable resume after master restart to prevent stale state
        disableResume()
        // Timestamps for audit trail
        timestamps()
    }

    environment {
        // Use Jenkins credentials binding - never hardcode secrets
        // Credentials are only exposed to the specific steps that need them
        DEPLOY_CREDS = credentials('production-deploy-credentials')
        // Restrict artifact registry credentials scope
        REGISTRY_CREDS = credentials('container-registry-credentials')
    }

    stages {
        stage('Checkout') {
            steps {
                // Clean workspace before checkout to prevent poisoned file attacks
                cleanWs()
                checkout scm
            }
        }

        stage('Build') {
            steps {
                // Run build in a controlled environment
                // Use --no-daemon to avoid long-running processes on the agent
                sh '''
                    set -euo pipefail
                    echo "Building application..."
                    chmod +x ./gradlew 2>/dev/null || true
                    ./gradlew clean build --no-daemon --no-build-cache
                '''
            }
        }

        stage('Security Scan') {
            steps {
                // Run security scanning before tests/deploy
                sh '''
                    set -euo pipefail
                    echo "Running dependency vulnerability scan..."
                    ./gradlew dependencyCheckAnalyze --no-daemon || true
                '''
                // Scan for secrets accidentally committed
                sh '''
                    set -euo pipefail
                    echo "Scanning for leaked secrets..."
                    if command -v gitleaks &> /dev/null; then
                        gitleaks detect --source . --no-git --verbose
                    else
                        echo "WARNING: gitleaks not installed on agent, skipping secret scan"
                    fi
                '''
            }
        }

        stage('Test') {
            steps {
                sh '''
                    set -euo pipefail
                    echo "Running tests..."
                    ./gradlew test --no-daemon
                '''
            }
            post {
                always {
                    // Archive test results for visibility
                    junit allowEmptyResults: true, testResults: '**/build/test-results/**/*.xml'
                }
            }
        }

        stage('Build Container Image') {
            steps {
                sh '''
                    set -euo pipefail
                    echo "Building container image..."
                    # Use a non-root user in the container image
                    # Pin base image by digest to prevent supply chain attacks
                    docker build \
                        --no-cache \
                        --pull \
                        -t "myapp:${BUILD_NUMBER}" \
                        -f Dockerfile .
                '''
            }
        }

        stage('Container Security Scan') {
            steps {
                sh '''
                    set -euo pipefail
                    echo "Scanning container image for vulnerabilities..."
                    if command -v trivy &> /dev/null; then
                        trivy image --exit-code 1 --severity HIGH,CRITICAL "myapp:${BUILD_NUMBER}"
                    else
                        echo "WARNING: trivy not installed on agent, skipping container scan"
                    fi
                '''
            }
        }

        stage('Deploy to Production') {
            // Require manual approval before production deployment
            when {
                branch 'main'
            }
            steps {
                // Manual approval gate with timeout
                timeout(time: 15, unit: 'MINUTES') {
                    input message: 'Deploy to production?',
                          ok: 'Deploy',
                          submitter: 'deploy-approvers'
                }

                // Use withCredentials to limit credential exposure scope
                // Credentials are masked in logs automatically
                withCredentials([
                    usernamePassword(
                        credentialsId: 'production-deploy-credentials',
                        usernameVariable: 'DEPLOY_USER',
                        passwordVariable: 'DEPLOY_TOKEN'
                    ),
                    usernamePassword(
                        credentialsId: 'container-registry-credentials',
                        usernameVariable: 'REGISTRY_USER',
                        passwordVariable: 'REGISTRY_PASS'
                    )
                ]) {
                    sh '''
                        set -euo pipefail

                        # Push image to registry
                        echo "${REGISTRY_PASS}" | docker login registry.example.com \
                            --username "${REGISTRY_USER}" \
                            --password-stdin

                        docker tag "myapp:${BUILD_NUMBER}" \
                            "registry.example.com/myapp:${BUILD_NUMBER}"
                        docker push "registry.example.com/myapp:${BUILD_NUMBER}"

                        # Deploy using credentials (masked in logs)
                        echo "Deploying to production..."
                        kubectl set image deployment/myapp \
                            myapp="registry.example.com/myapp:${BUILD_NUMBER}" \
                            --namespace=production \
                            --record

                        # Wait for rollout to complete
                        kubectl rollout status deployment/myapp \
                            --namespace=production \
                            --timeout=300s

                        # Logout from registry
                        docker logout registry.example.com
                    '''
                }
            }
        }
    }

    post {
        always {
            // Clean workspace to remove any sensitive data
            cleanWs(
                cleanWhenNotBuilt: true,
                deleteDirs: true,
                disableDeferredWipeout: true,
                notFailBuild: true
            )
            // Remove local docker images to prevent disk bloat and data leakage
            sh '''
                docker rmi "myapp:${BUILD_NUMBER}" 2>/dev/null || true
                docker rmi "registry.example.com/myapp:${BUILD_NUMBER}" 2>/dev/null || true
            '''
        }
        success {
            echo 'Pipeline completed successfully.'
        }
        failure {
            echo 'Pipeline failed. Review logs for details.'
            // Notify team on failure - avoid including sensitive details in notifications
            // mail to: 'team@example.com',
            //      subject: "FAILED: ${env.JOB_NAME} #${env.BUILD_NUMBER}",
            //      body: "Build failed. Check: ${env.BUILD_URL}"
        }
    }
}
